title: SEC Announces Enforcement Results for Fiscal Year 2024
fullText: The Securities and Exchange Commission today announced that it filed 583 total enforcement actions in fiscal year 2024 and obtained orders for $8.2 billion in financial remedies, the highest amount in SEC history. The Commission also obtained orders barring 124 individuals from serving as officers and directors of public companies. The enforcement program prioritized investor protection, market integrity, and accountability for individuals. Cases addressed misconduct involving accounting fraud, market manipulation, insider trading, custody and recordkeeping violations by registrants, and abuses of emerging technologies including AI-washing and crypto asset fraud. The Division also pursued more than 200 actions for off-channel communications recordkeeping failures by broker-dealers and investment advisers.

title: SEC Charges Investment Adviser With Failing to Address AI Disclosure Controls
fullText: The Securities and Exchange Commission today charged a registered investment adviser with failing to implement reasonably designed compliance policies and procedures concerning the firm's use of artificial intelligence in its investment process. According to the order, the adviser publicly represented that its investment recommendations were generated by proprietary AI models, but failed to maintain documentation of model training data, validation protocols, or performance monitoring. The order finds that this conduct violated Section 206(4) of the Investment Advisers Act of 1940 and Rule 206(4)-7 thereunder. Without admitting or denying the findings, the firm agreed to a cease-and-desist order, censure, a $400,000 civil penalty, and undertakings to enhance its AI governance program.
